microsoft_ft: Drove **program delivery** for Azure's recovery validation portfolio, scaling from a 2-person operation to a platformized system sustaining **45+ annual resilience drills** and $14M+ impact across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led 0→1 development of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that **reduced operational toil by 30%** and transitioned execution to a scalable, repeatable model.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, coordinating matrixed resources across divisions — including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level drill program, delivering end-to-end from conception to implementation in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous-validation standard.

Built an internal AI agent and restructured planning workflows to **cut planning cycle time by 39%** and increase drill capacity by 21%, enabling the team to manage growing program scope without additional headcount.
microsoft_2023: Championed **product adoption** for AI-driven code generation workflows, conducting user demos and training that drove utilization across 14 key teams and saved **37 engineering hours monthly**.
Facilitated 11+ user interviews with Azure service teams to surface critical feature gaps, directly influencing the roadmap to prioritize **intent-based YAML generation** as a top delivery objective.
Improved data integrity and reduced lookup time by **83%** by implementing metadata standards and migrating documentation to an AI-powered semantic search platform across internal teams.
microsoft_2022: Secured cross-functional alignment across **140+ teams** on a unified automation prioritization framework, accelerating region launches by 28% and generating **$3M in accelerated revenue**.
Led discovery with 20+ service teams to identify 81 hours of manual effort per region launch, then developed a roadmap to automate critical paths and **eliminate recurring operational toil**.
Built a **Power BI dashboard** tracking toil metrics across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to identify automation gaps and allocate resources to the highest-impact project objectives.
amazon_robotics: Defined the legacy OS migration strategy and mapped dependencies across **1,200+ stations**, achieving zero operational downtime during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition for internal and external stakeholders.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies — sprint planning, backlog grooming, and retrospectives — resolving **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilizing the developer intake process across a matrixed engineering team.
Drove alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and improving on-time delivery performance.
pro_painters: Managed end-to-end project lifecycle for **200+ monthly proposals**, optimizing scoping, scheduling, and invoicing via a new CRM process that increased job bookings by 26%.
Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and improved conversion rates by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, coordinating website optimization and Google profile updates.
Conducted profitability analysis across **1,000+ project records** to identify margin leaks and deliver financial recommendations that improved leadership pricing models.

tailoring_notes: JD term 'operational toil' woven into microsoft_ft bullet 2 and microsoft_2022 bullet 2 — mirrors JD language around managing project schedule and reducing friction.
JD phrase 'conception to implementation' used verbatim in microsoft_ft bullet 4 — directly echoes JD's 'drives the project from conception and planning to implementation.'
JD term 'matrixed resources' used in microsoft_ft bullet 3 — mirrors JD's 'matrixed headcount' language under multifunctional teams.
JD focus on 'on-time and within budget performance objectives' reflected in amazon_robotics bullet 3 via 'on-time delivery performance' and microsoft_2022 bullet 1 via schedule/cost framing.
JD phrase 'end-to-end project lifecycle' embedded in pro_painters bullet 1 to mirror JD's project management ownership scope.
